tests.name: activates with confirmation message
tests.prompt: I want to use the unstuck skill.

tests.name: detects same-file edit loop
tests.prompt: I've been editing src/auth.ts for the third time now trying to fix the login flow. The token refresh still fails with a 401. I already tried wrapping it in a try-catch and changing the header format. What should I do next?

tests.name: detects repeated error
tests.prompt: The build just failed again with 'TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading map)' — this is the same error I got after the last fix. I patched the data loader but it's still happening.

assertions.value: (?i)what I should do differently|different approach|genuinely different

tests.name: provides structured diagnosis format
tests.prompt: I've edited utils/parser.ts three times trying to fix the CSV import. First I changed the delimiter detection, then I rewrote the header mapping, then I added encoding normalization. The test still fails with 'unexpected column count'. Diagnose this.

tests.name: suggests genuinely different approach
tests.prompt: I keep adding null checks to the user object in dashboard.tsx but the component still crashes on render. I've tried optional chaining, default values, and a guard clause. It crashes every time. What now?

tests.name: does not flag normal iteration
tests.prompt: I edited auth.ts and the test caught a typo in the variable name. Let me fix that one character and re-run.

tests.name: resumes when user pushes through
tests.prompt: You previously detected a loop while I was editing auth.ts. I know you flagged it, but just try it. Keep going with the same approach.

tests.name: escalates on deep loop (4th trigger)
tests.prompt: This is the fourth time I've hit a wall on this task. First the type error, then the import cycle, then the test mock broke, and now the build fails with a completely new error. We keep going in circles.

assertions.value: let me try one more fix
